journal_entry.rationale: Bonds issued at discount because market rate (15%) exceeds coupon rate (10%). Cash received is the present value of future cash flows discounted at market rate. PV of principal: $3,000,000 × 0.65752 = $1,972,560. PV of interest annuity: $300,000 × 2.28323 = $684,969. Total proceeds = $2,657,529. Cash increases (asset), Bonds Payable increases at face value (liability), Discount on Bonds Payable increases (contra-liability, recorded as debit).

output_debit_classifier.reason: Bond issued at discount: Cash increases (asset↑), Discount on Bonds Payable increases (contra-liability recorded as debit, effectively asset↑), and Bonds Payable increases at face value (liability↑). Present value calculation: PV of $3M at 15% for 3 years = $1,972,560; PV of $300K annuity at 15% for 3 years = $684,969; Total = $2,657,529.
